full_text:
Progress with Expansion and Future plans - Nika

70 samples from 65 patients were initially selected for Expansion tCyCIF.
3 samples were excluded from the analysis (2 due to poor quality, 1 due to data loss).
Final cohort: 67 samples from 62 patients. 45 samples will be integrated with GeoMx.


Experimental assessments have been completed
Channel that didn’t work: CD3D
Challenging channels: ZIC2, PAX8, p-RPA32, Cyclin E1

Expansion tCyCIF - S482 (also in Xenium cohort)

Serous Borderline Tumor like architecture

How do our project focus, questions and aims differ?

Xenium + scRNAseq
IDS cancer cell phenotype identification
Spatial distribution of the RNA-level phenotypes
Signature identification for each phenotype
Xenium + tCycIF
Protein-level phenotype characterization
Protein-RNA correlations
Are tCycIF-based phenotypes same as Xenium?
Trajectory analyses
Bulk / scRNAseq
Validate signatures and clinical correlations in a larger cohort

Questions Cell-cycle

Questions Cavachon

Xenium + tCycIF
Integration - does it work?
RNA + protein
Global
Separate compartment (nucleus/cyto)
Is adding morphology providing new information?
Can we see new populations?
Can we integrate Cell-cell interaction?

Delineate spatially the cancer phenotypes in IDS samples
Which of them might impact chemotherapy response?
Characterize them at the protein and RNA-level
Identifying clinically-meaningful mechanisms to validate in other cohorts

CLINICAL/BIOLOGICAL FOCUS, CANCER-CELLS

METHODOLOGICAL/TECHNICAL FOCUS

Plans for Xenium in Cell-Cycle and Cavachon projects

Advancements with image processing + alignment

Xenium explorer visualization

Automatic combined placement

Loads xenium masks, clusters & transcripts, now also cycif images

Step by step process for the alignment + scripts 
Documented in a guideline
All run in QuPath in a workstation (~7h per image)

Initial observations

Xenium - Computational Resources

Xenium data is small

CycIF is huge as usual

Data duplicity: tif, cropped tif, aligned tif…

CycIF works OK on workstations
CSC proved to be too messy to install/run our tools
QuPath alignment requires GUI

Xenium data can run well in workstations and high-RAM laptops

HPC needed for
integration// scRNAseq

Bulk RNA-seq Deconvolution (Discovery t-Cycif)

PRISM deconvolution in 26 samples inside iCAN platform

Initial Observations
Deconvolution isolates well different compartments
Archetypes and gene expression match what seen at protein level

Can we deconvolute broader tumor functional signatures?
Can we extend these findings to more patients?

Advancements with scRNA-seq

N = 5 samples (4 PDS, 1 IDS), 3 gels
ncells initial: 25010
assigned cells to samples: 17050
ncells after QC (quite stringent): 12770

Advancements with scRNA-seq

Are we able to identify the functional clusters that we were seeing before?

Next steps with scRNA-seq/Xenium

Predict and transfer the location of the dissociated cells
Predict spatial location of unmeasured genes

Spatial domains (expression + shape) to characterize tissue architecture
